title: CSV, JSON Lines, Schemas, Stable Output, Redaction, and Accessible Reports · Language Clinic: Csv Contract
summary: Columns reorder between runs, newlines corrupt CSV, None becomes ambiguous, JSON arrays cannot stream, secrets enter logs, and the visual report has no table semantics. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the csv contract increment in versioned machine and accessible human report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.
Encode HTML reports, neutralize spreadsheet formulas, validate URLs, escape logs, and keep untrusted content out of commands and templates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-csv-contract — Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-export-accessible-reports-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-export-accessible-reports-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_export_accessible_reports_3(valid_records: int = 48, csv_rows: int = 48, jsonl_rows: int = 48, accessible_findings: int = 48, leaked_sensitive_fields: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if valid_records < 1 or csv_rows < 0 or jsonl_rows < 0:
        return False, valid_records, "export-count"
    if csv_rows != valid_records or jsonl_rows != valid_records:
        return False, csv_rows, "export-reconciliation"
    if accessible_findings != valid_records:
        return False, accessible_findings, "accessible-report-gap"
    if leaked_sensitive_fields > 0:
        return False, leaked_sensitive_fields, "redaction-gap"
    assert csv_rows == valid_records and jsonl_rows == valid_records
    return True, csv_rows, "stable-accessible-reports"

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory site-quality team handles case 1007 during a local-server failure rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile stable CSV and JSON Lines exports, accessible human reports, provenance, and redaction; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Joining values with commas produces portable CSV.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement. Artifact under review: versioned machine and accessible human report.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Csv Contract, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Csv Contract record says versioned machine and accessible human report looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect csv contract evidence boundary for Csv Contract.

For inspect csv contract evidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Csv Contract for versioned machine and accessible human report.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Csv Contract: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 2626 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile stable CSV and JSON Lines exports, accessible human reports, provenance, and redaction; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract.
steps.why: Retrieving Csv Contract through the concrete versioned machine and accessible human report case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract, keep Csv Contract connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Csv Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 2626 during an authorized nonprofit content audit. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define UTF-8, newline handling, dialect, ordered fields, quoting, formula policy, missing values, limits, and atomic replacement.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Csv Contract model · Theory Csv Contract, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Joining values with commas produces portable CSV.
